Alhambra, Generalife and Albayzín, Granada

Rising over modern Granada, the Alhambra and the Albayzín, situated on two adjacent hills, form the medieval part of the city. To the east of the Alhambra fortress and residence are the magnificent gardens of the Generalife, the former rural residence of the emirs who ruled this part of Spain in the 13th and 14th centuries.

The Alhambra was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1984 under criteria (i), (iii) and (iv). The property covers an area of approximately 142 hectares.

The Albayzín, of Moorish origin, preserves narrow streets and Arab foundations as well as Renaissance and Baroque additions, all in harmony with the Andalusian architecture.
